ops.title: capt-hook 5.0.0 imports WorkflowCall (cc-transcript 7.1.0+) but pins cc-transcript>=7,<8, admitting WorkflowCall-less 7.0.x — an ImportError trap
ops.body:
capt-hook 5.0.0's `conditions.py` does `from cc_transcript.tools import SkillCall, WorkflowCall, tool_name_matches`. `WorkflowCall` only exists in **cc-transcript 7.1.0**, but capt-hook 5.0.0 declares `cc-transcript>=7,<8`, which also admits 7.0.0/7.0.1/7.0.2 — all lacking `WorkflowCall`. Any resolver that lands on 7.0.x fails at capt-hook import with `ImportError: cannot import name 'WorkflowCall' from 'cc_transcript.tools'`. The correct pin is `cc-transcript>=7.1`.

**Why it matters (latent prod risk):** cc-notes' `.claude/settings.json` runs `uvx capt-hook run <event>` UNPINNED. uvx's fresh resolution picks 7.1.0 (highest) so prod is fine today, but a stale uv preference or an exclude-newer cutoff can drop it to 7.0.x and break every hook event. Fix belongs upstream in capt-hook ([[capt-hook-is-cc-notes-hooks-upstream]] = ~/Code/captain-hook) — tighten the `cc-transcript` lower bound to `>=7.1`.

Surfaced during the capt-hook 4.5.0→5.0.0 pack migration (the only pack rename was `command.Command` → `ParsedCommand`; `types.Command` is a distinct condition class that stayed). The bare `uv run` helper-test command hit this via a stale uv preference — see [[uv-exclude-newer-hides-fresh-publishes]] for the `--upgrade-package`/`--no-cache` workaround.
